metrics.name: Banxico
metrics.question: Will the Banxico Governing Board's June 25, 2026, interest rate decision be unanimous (5-0 vote)?

provenance.quantity: Banxico Governing Board vote is unanimous (5-0)
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

provenance.law: D7 faithful roster + D8 grounded initial positions + D14 convergence + seat-weighted absolute-threshold tally

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

simulation_result.question: Will the Banxico Governing Board's June 25, 2026, interest rate decision be unanimous (5-0 vote)?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.8333 (counted n=2); simulation-conditional 0.4398 (resolved mass 1.00); headline 0.4398 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
dependence_sensitive: the actor-state joint dependence is unidentified; the answer moves across (0.0833, 1.0) between the independent and comonotonic structures — no single correlation was assumed
bounded omitted-state residual 0.143: the interval widens to [0.0, 0.4284] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: The May 7, 2026, 3-2 split revealed deep internal disagreement, with Heath and Espinosa preferring a hold. The board declared the easing cycle over, so the June meeting tests whether the majority can unify on a hold or if dissenters persist, potentially joined by others if growth concerns or inflati

terminal_canonicalization.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

easing_cycle_ended.claim: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over in May 2026, shifting to a data-dependent hold stance.

provenance.definition: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over in May 2026, shifting to a data-dependent hold stance.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico ends easing cycle with split vote, signaling future holds.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank declared an end to its two-year easing cycle.

internal_dissent_on_hold.claim: Two board members (Heath, Espinosa) dissented from the cut, preferring to hold, indicating a hawkish minority.

provenance.definition: Two board members (Heath, Espinosa) dissented from the cut, preferring to hold, indicating a hawkish minority.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Heath and Espinosa voted to hold rate at 6.75% while majority cut to 6.50%.
cases.basis_quote: Board members Jonathan Heath and Irene Espinosa Cantellano dissented, preferring to hold the rate at 6.75%.

economic_contraction_and_inflation_above.claim: Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast revised upward to 4.1% for Q2 2026, above 3% target.

provenance.definition: Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast revised upward to 4.1% for Q2 2026, above 3% target.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico cites contraction and upward inflation revision in May 2026 statement.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank cited a contraction in economic activity and revised its inflation forecast for Q2 2026 upward to 4.1%.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Banxico Governing Board vote is unanimous (5-0)

provenance.definition: All five members vote for the same policy action (hold, cut, or hike) with no dissents.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official statement or minutes report a 5-0 vote for the policy action.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any dissent (4-1, 3-2, etc.), meeting cancellation, or postponement beyond July 1, 2026.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico's March 2025 meeting had a unanimous 5-0 vote to hold rate.
cases.basis_quote: The Governing Board decided unanimously to keep the target for the overnight interbank funding rate at 9.00%.

cases.description: Banxico's February 2024 meeting had a unanimous 5-0 vote to hold rate.
cases.basis_quote: The Governing Board decided unanimously to keep the target for the overnight interbank funding rate at 11.25%.

banxico_gb.quorum: At least three members must be present to vote.
banxico_gb.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If meeting is cancelled or postponed beyond July 1, 2026, question resolves No.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

victoria_rodriguez_ceja.claim: Victoria believes that the economic contraction is severe enough to warrant a rate cut despite inflation being above target. She views the easing cycle as having ended prematurely and thinks a cut now can boost growth without triggering a spiral, given that inflation is only moderately above target.
beliefs: The contraction is worse than official data suggests
Inflation will fall as demand weakens

irene_espinosa_cantellano.claim: Irene believes that despite easing_cycle_ended and economic_contraction_and_inflation_above_target, a premature cut would signal panic and undermine Banxico's inflation-fighting credibility. She sees internal_dissent_on_hold as a reason to double down on a hold to prove the board's resolve.
beliefs: Inflation expectations remain unanchored; a cut would be seen as capitulation.
The contraction is temporary and supply-driven; demand-side easing won't help.
A unanimous hold is the only way to maintain institutional credibility.
Irene believes that despite easing_cycle_ended and economic_contraction_and_inflation_above_target, a premature cut would signal panic and undermine Banxico's inflation-fighting credibility.
She sees internal_dissent_on_hold as a reason to double down on a hold to prove the board's resolve.
Internal dissent from doves is a threat to the board's cohesion;
she must persuade or outvote them.
goals: Preserve Banxico's reputation as an inflation hawk.
Avoid any action that could be interpreted as political pressure.

distinguishing_observations: She would emphasize forward guidance language that keeps a tightening bias.
She would argue that cutting now would repeat the mistake of 2015 when Banxico cut too early.

irene_espinosa_cantellano.claim: Irene privately concludes that the economic_contraction_and_inflation_above_target is now a demand-side crisis, and that holding rates is exacerbating the downturn. She believes easing_cycle_ended was premature and that a bold cut is needed to stimulate growth, even if it means breaking unanimity.
beliefs: Inflation is being driven by supply shocks that will fade; demand is collapsing.
The real economy is in worse shape than the board acknowledges.
A cut now could be reversed later if inflation resurges; inaction is riskier.

omar_mejia_castelazo.claim: Omar interprets the easing cycle as still ongoing and believes the contraction justifies a cut to support growth, even with inflation above target. He views the internal dissent as a sign that a cut is the correct path and will vote to cut, accepting a possible split.
beliefs: Economic contraction is the more urgent problem
Inflation above target is transitory
A cut can be justified as a growth-support measure

latent_beliefs: Inflation expectations are de-anchoring
A cut would be premature and damage Banxico's reputation

Victoria believes that after the easing cycle ended, inflation remains above target and the economy is contracting, so holding rates is the only way to signal commitment to price stability and prevent a wage-price spiral.
She sees internal dissent as a risk to Banxico's credibility and will use her
Market expectations for a cut are high, but she prioritizes long-term credibility over short-term relief.
latent_preferences: Opposes any dissent that weakens the board's message
public_facts: (reported) [2025-03-27] Banxico's March 2025 meeting had a unanimous 5-0 vote to hold rate. — “The Governing Board decided unanimously to keep the target for the overnight interbank funding rate at 9.00%.”

(reported) [2024-02-08] Banxico's February 2024 meeting had a unanimous 5-0 vote to hold rate. — “The Governing Board decided unanimously to keep the target for the overnight interbank funding rate at 11.25%.”
(confirmed) Historical frequency of unanimous votes at Banxico (approximate) — “The 3-2 split reflects meaningful internal disagreement on the board.”
(confirmed) [2026-05-07] Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over in May 2026, shifting to a data-dependent hold stance. — “The central bank declared an end to its two-year easing cycle.”

(confirmed) [2026-05-07] Two board members (Heath, Espinosa) dissented from the cut, preferring to hold, indicating a hawkish minority. — “Board members Jonathan Heath and Irene Espinosa Cantellano dissented, preferring to hold the rate at 6.75%.”

(confirmed) [2026-05-07] Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast revised upward to 4.1% for Q2 2026, above 3% target. — “The central bank cited a contraction in economic activity and revised its inflation forecast for Q2 2026 upward to 4.1%.”

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: behavioral replicates not requested (deterministic execution: content-addressed draws make replicate 0 exhaustive)

provenance.quantity: Banxico Governing Board vote is unanimous (5-0)
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

provenance.law: D7 faithful roster + D8 grounded initial positions + D14 convergence + seat-weighted absolute-threshold tally

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

record_vote.description: Each board member records their vote for a policy action.

publish_statement.description: Banxico publishes official press release with vote tally.
